text:
Amador Patient Navigator | Amador County
Skip to Main Content
Connecting Care. Connecting Community.
The Amador Patient Navigator Program is a free service from Amador County Public Health, in partnership with Sutter Health, Anthem, Health Net, and Kaiser Permanente, designed to connect residents with the support they need most. Whether it’s medical resources, finding transportation, or accessing food and community resources, our Patient Navigator provides one-on-one guidance to reduce barriers and and improves access to care. It’s about helping people get connected, stay healthy, and move forward—one step at a time.
Helpful services include:
Connecting uninsured/underinsured  people with primary care doctor
Schedule appointments for patients with a new primary care provider
Provide reminder calls for first primary care visit
Arrange transportation
Connect to community resources (housing, food, etc.)
